description: Fail-loud: chronon3d_cli render must abort loudly when the output path is non-writable (e.g., /root/ for a normal user, /etc/shadow.mp4). Canonical error vocabulary: Composition/Path/Output/Write tokens in stderr, NO silent fallback markers, exit code != 0, output MP4 absent.
honesty_note: Tested on POSIX systems where /etc/passwd is read-only for the running user. On Windows the path collapses to the system root. Per AGENTS.md §honesty: the bash script must NOT silently fall back if the output path disappears for OS-level reasons (e.g., sandbox without /etc/) — if the path is not writable for ANY reason, the CLI should exit non-zero with stderr explaining the write failure.
